# Title: Seller Fee Tax Invoice

Title: Seller Fee Tax Invoice

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G202162720

Markdown Content:
Under Mexican tax authorities rules, Amazon Mexico is required to issue and submit tax invoices (CFDI) to the government for all applicable transactions, including any fees we charge or reverse to third-party sellers on Amazon Mexico’s Marketplace.

Only sellers who have a valid RFC Tax ID (Número del Registro Federal de Contribuyentes) and have provided this information to Amazon on Seller Central will receive individual tax invoices.

Amazon will issue tax invoices (CFDI) by the third day of the month for all fees charged and/or reversed during the prior month. There will be one invoice for all fees charged, and one for any adjustments or reversal of fees. Each fee type will be aggregated individually on each tax invoice. Tax invoices are created in both XML and PDF formats and will be available for download on Seller Central. Note that the XML is the legally valid document, and the PDF is a user-friendly version of the same.

**Note:**Regular Seller Fees will be included in the Seller Fee Tax Invoice. MSS Shipping Fees —if applicable— will be included in a separate invoice. [](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G202162720)

## To access current and previous Mexican Tax Invoices:

1.   Sign in to Seller Central from the desktop version.
2.   Under the **Reports** menu, select **Tax Document Library**.
3.   In the **Tax Document Library**, select **Mexican Tax Invoices**.

Tax invoices will be available in the **Mexican Tax Invoices** library for up to 24 months after the corresponding period. In case you require older invoices or in case of further assistance, please contact us via Seller Central.

## To download Mexican Tax Invoices:

1.   Identify the invoice you want to download.
2.   Click **PDF** or **XML** to download the desired format.
3.   Repeat this process for every invoice you wish to download.

For sellers who have not registered an RFC Tax ID with Amazon, we will report their transactions to the tax authorities in a consolidated tax invoice.

## To add or update your RFC Tax ID information:

1.   Sign in to Seller Central, and then click **Settings**.
2.   On **Seller Account Information**, under the **Tax Information** section and click **RFC ID**.
3.   Add or edit your RFC ID and then click **Submit**. 

**Note:**Once you provide the RFC ID, you can only edit it. However, you won't be able to delete the RFC number.

**Note:**Once you provide the RFC ID, you can only edit it. However, you won't be able to delete the RFC number.

## Frequently Asked Questions

#### What is an electronic tax invoice (factura electrónica) or a CFDI?

Comprobante Fiscal Digital por Internet (CFDI) is an electronic document in XML format that is digitally stamped by the Mexican Tax authorities for income tax and VAT purposes. We are required to issue and submit to the government tax invoices (CFDI) for all VAT-applicable transactions, including for any fees we charge or reverse to sellers on Amazon Mexico.

#### Which sellers receive individual tax invoices?

#### Which sellers receive individual tax invoices?

For sellers who are registered with the Mexican Tax authorities (SAT) with an RFC Tax ID (Número del Registro Federal de Contribuyentes) and that have provided this information to Amazon, we issue the tax invoices against the RFC Tax ID that we have on file. These sellers are considered "domestic" sellers for tax purposes. We will issue the tax invoices (CFDI) to the RFC Tax ID that you provided us. We do not validate the accuracy RFC Tax ID, so please make sure you verify it when you provide it.

For those sellers who do not have an RFC Tax ID registered with us (these are considered “foreign” sellers), we report their transactions to the tax authorities in a consolidated tax invoice.

#### How can I download Mexican Tax Invoices in the Mobile version of Seller Central or through the Amazon Seller app?

Currently Mexican Tax Invoices are only available for download on the desktop version of Seller Central.

Currently Mexican Tax Invoices are only available for download on the desktop version of Seller Central.

#### How can I get more information?

In case you have any questions, review the Help content in Seller Central for how VAT is calculated, charged and/or collected. In case of further assistance, contact us via Seller Central.
